content: The human language learning concept of reflexion as explicit vocabulary and metacognitive practice can be borrowed to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms: structured, scaffolded reflexion training similar to how high school students learn reflexion as a deliberate skill can reduce inconsistent self-evaluation in large language models. This frames current LLM self-reflection research's struggle with inconsistent perspective alignment as an untrained skill that can be taught through graded, explicit practice rather than just being engineered into model architectures.
